---
title: "4.6 CSV Split and PokeImages"
subtitle: "Using Text Files"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Split Strings

### CSV rows contain multiple attributes

If you look at the Pokemon CSV file, each line contains multiple data attributes separated by commas.

These attributes include each Pokemon's name, type, speed, and other fields on each row.

Typically, the first line of a CSV file serves as the header, indicating the names of these attributes.

```text
Number, Pokemon, Type 1, Type 2, HP, Attack, Defense, Speed, PNG, Description
```

## split(String delimiter)

### Delimiter separates fields

The Java `String` class provides a useful method called `split(String delimiter)`.

It splits a string into an array of substrings based on a specified **delimiter** (分隔符), such as a comma or a space.

This method returns a `String` array where each element represents a field of data from the line.

```java
String sentence = "A quick brown fox jumps";
String[] words = sentence.split(" ");
```

## Split a Pokemon Row

### Use indexes from the header

The first line of headers indicates that element `0` is the Pokemon's number, element `1` is the name, and so on.

We only need to save the data that we want to use.

```java
// Split the line of data into an array of Strings
String[] data = line.split(",");
// data: Number,Name,Type1,Type2,HP,Attack,Defense,Speed,PNG,Description
String name = data[1];
String type1 = data[2];
String speed = data[7];
String imageFile = data[8];
```

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: pokeImages`

**PokeImages:** This program reads in some of the data from the pokemon file into a `String` array of lines.

Complete the `randomPokemon` method to print out a random pokemon name and its image using the `split` method.

Run the program multiple times to see different Pokemon names and images.

## PokeImages Fields

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
import java.io.*;
import java.util.*;

public class PokeImages
{
    private String filename = "pokemon.csv";
    private String[] pokemonLines = new String[152];

    /* This method reads in filename into the pokemonLines array */
    public int readFile() throws IOException
    {
        File myFile = new File(filename);
        Scanner scan = new Scanner(myFile);
        int i = 0;
        while (scan.hasNext())
        {
            pokemonLines[i] = scan.nextLine();
            i++;
        }
        System.out.println("Read in " + i + " lines.");
        scan.close();
        return i;
    }
```
:::

## randomPokemon Starter

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
    /* Write a function randomPokemon that prints out a random Pokemon name and image */
    public void randomPokemon(int length)
    {
        // 1. pick a random number from 1 to length
        //    (the 0th row is the headers)


        // 2. get the line at that random index from the array pokemonLines

        // 3. Use the split method to split the line into a String array data

        // 4. Print out the name using the correct index in the split data
        //   (Check above for the correct index for the name)

        // 5. Call the printHTMLimage method below
        //    with an element of the data array to print out the image.
        //    (Check above for the correct index for the image url)

    }
```
:::

## PokeImages Main and Image Method

```java
    public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException
    {
        PokeImages obj = new PokeImages();
        // call readFile() to read file into the array pokemonLines
        int length = obj.readFile();
        obj.randomPokemon(length);
    }

    // This method will just work on Runestone to print out images
    public static void printHTMLimage(String url)
    {
        System.out.print("<img src=" + url + " width=300px />");
    }
}
```

## PokeImages Test Targets

### Runestone checks

The tests check that student code includes:

::: {.tight-list}
- `(int) (Math.random()*length) + 1`
- a call to `.split`
- a call to `printHTMLimage(`
- the correct index for the name: `[1]`
- the correct index for the image url: `[8]`
:::

## Object-Oriented Design with CSV Files

### Rows become objects

To better organize and work with this data, we can create a `Pokemon` class that corresponds to these attributes using object-oriented design.

A CSV data file can be saved into an array of `Pokemon` objects by splitting each line, except the header, into the attributes for one `Pokemon` object.

## CSV to Pokemon Objects

### Use split, then constructor

Assuming that we have already written the `Pokemon` class and constructor, this code creates a Pokemon object from the data and saves it into an array of `Pokemon` objects.

```java
// Create an array of Pokemon objects
Pokemon[] pokemonArray = new Pokemon[152];

int i = 0;
while (scan.hasNext())
{
    String line = scan.nextLine();
    // Split each line into its attributes name, type1, etc.
    String[] data = line.split(",");
    String name = data[1];
    String type1 = data[2];
    String speed = data[7];
    String imageFile = data[8];
```

## CSV to Pokemon Objects

### Construct and store the object

```java
// Create a Pokemon object from the split data
Pokemon p = new Pokemon(name, type1, speed, imageFile);
// Save p in the array
pokemonArray[i] = p;

i++;
```

The object array stores the `Pokemon` reference after the row has been split into fields.

## Header Row Note

### Sometimes row 0 should be skipped

Sometimes you may need to skip the 0th row in the file if it is column headers.

For this file, it will just get split into strings.

If your data file contains numbers that need to be processed, you could skip one like:

```java
if (i == 0) scan.nextLine();
```

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- identify the CSV header row and field indexes
- explain that `split(",")` returns a `String[]`
- use `[1]` for Pokemon name and `[8]` for image url in this source file
- generate a random index that skips the header row
- convert each CSV line into fields before constructing an object
- store constructed objects into an array with an index counter
:::

## End

### 4.6 Part 2 complete

Next: Pokemon object array challenge.
